description_long: This object is a classic CRT-style computer monitor with a deep, boxy rear section and a flat, square display area. The front features a thick, matte black bezel framing the screen, with a small control panel below the display that includes three triangular buttons in yellow and red. The sides and rear are solid and angular, with the back protruding outward in a trapezoidal shape typical of older monitors. The surface appears to be made of hard plastic with a smooth, slightly matte finish. No visible ports or branding are present, and the monitor sits directly on its base without a separate stand. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, suitable for public or semi-industrial urban environments.
description: A boxy, black CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, deep rear section, and a small control panel with colored buttons below the screen.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat display, thick bezel, and three colored buttons below the screen.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the monitor's deep, rectangular profile and protruding rear section.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the trapezoidal back extension and solid, unadorned surface.
description_view_3: Right side view mirroring the left, emphasizing the depth and angular rear of the monitor.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and matte black, with sharp corners and no decorative elements or color contrast. It frames the display area with a utilitarian, robust appearance.
attribute_stand_type: No separate stand is visible; the monitor's base is integrated and flat, forming a continuous extension of the main body.
attribute_button_placement: Three triangular buttons are located on the bottom front panel, directly below the screen, grouped together in a horizontal row.
